Controls.Title: All resources require authentication
Controls.Finding: Microsoft Entra ID treats all applications as resources requiring authentication. No on-premises bypass paths exist in the defined CA scope.

Controls.Title: All communications secured (TLS/OAuth2)
Controls.Finding: All Microsoft Graph API calls use TLS 1.2+. OAuth 2.0 client_credentials flow used for service-to-service auth. No HTTP endpoints in use.

Controls.Title: Per-session access with re-authentication
Controls.Finding: CA006 enforces 8-hour session limits with no persistent browser sessions. CA007 enforces 30-minute sessions for privileged IT accounts.

Controls.Title: Dynamic policy enforcement based on risk signals
Controls.Finding: CA004 blocks high sign-in risk. CA008 blocks high user risk. CA009 requires password change on medium/high user risk. Together these implement dynamic, risk-adaptive access control.

Controls.Title: Continuous asset and user integrity monitoring
Controls.Finding: Automated monitoring pipeline runs every 15 minutes via GitHub Actions. OSINT scan covers all 535 personas across 4 sources. 0 users currently flagged as credential-exposed. Device compliance tracked for 0 non-compliant devices.

Controls.Title: Strict authentication and authorisation before every access
Controls.Finding: MFA enforced for all users with no registration gaps.

Controls.Title: Continuous telemetry collection and posture improvement
Controls.Finding: Threat intelligence engine derives 7 prioritised recommendations per scan. Dashboard prediction model projects 6-month exposure trend. Audit logs retained in data/osint_results/osint_run.log. All scan results committed to version-controlled JSON for historical analysis.

Controls.Title: Account Management
Controls.Finding: 500 accounts provisioned via automated scripts with full lifecycle management. Joiner/Mover/Leaver workflows implemented. Leaver deprovisioning < 3 seconds. All accounts tied to verified department and role.

Controls.Title: Access Enforcement
Controls.Finding: 12 Conditional Access policies deployed. 1 enforced, 5 in Report-Only mode. Report-Only mode logs violations but does not block access — full enforcement pending operational sign-off.
Controls.Recommendation: Promote all CA policies from Report-Only to Enabled state to achieve full AC-3 compliance.

Controls.Title: Least Privilege
Controls.Finding: Privileged Identity Management (PIM) implemented for all 45 eligible admin assignments. Zero permanently active privileged roles. Role mapping follows least-privilege per job function: Help Desk → Helpdesk Admin only; Network Engineer → Reports Reader only.

Controls.Title: Least Privilege — Privileged Accounts
Controls.Finding: CA005 requires both MFA AND compliant device for IT department (AND operator). CA007 enforces 30-minute re-authentication for privileged accounts. Global Administrator role restricted to break-glass accounts only.
Evidence: CA005 — MFA AND compliant device for IT
Break-glass accounts excluded from CA scope

Controls.Title: Session Lock
Controls.Finding: CA006 enforces 8-hour sign-in frequency for all users with no persistent browser. CA007 enforces 30-minute re-authentication for IT/privileged accounts.

Controls.Title: Remote Access
Controls.Finding: All remote access governed by Conditional Access — location is irrelevant to policy enforcement. MFA required regardless of network. CA003 additionally requires compliant device for clinical staff accessing from any location.
Evidence: CA001 applies regardless of network location

Controls.Title: Event Logging
Controls.Finding: OSINT scan events logged to data/osint_results/osint_run.log with timestamps. Threat audit outputs to threat_audit.json with AuditDate. Entra ID sign-in logs available via Graph API but not currently exported to file. Recommend enabling Entra ID diagnostic settings to export sign-in logs.
Controls.Recommendation: Enable Entra ID diagnostic settings to export sign-in and audit logs to Log Analytics or storage.

Controls.Title: Audit Record Generation
Controls.Finding: Automated pipeline generates audit records every 15 minutes for threat signals and every 6 hours for OSINT. All records include timestamp, source, and findings. Sign-in audit records exist in Entra ID but are not pulled into the local audit store.
Controls.Recommendation: Extend entra_sync.py to pull sign-in logs and store in data/signin_logs/.

Controls.Title: Identification and Authentication (Org Users)
Controls.Finding: MFA enforced via CA001 for all users. CA005 enforces MFA + compliant device for admins. 0 users have not yet registered MFA — these accounts cannot fully satisfy IA-2 until registration is complete.

Controls.Title: MFA for Privileged Network Access
Controls.Finding: CA005 requires BOTH MFA and compliant device (AND operator) for all IT department privileged accounts. This exceeds minimum IA-2(1) which requires MFA alone.

Controls.Title: Authenticator Management — Breached Password Check
Controls.Finding: CA009 requires mandatory password change when user risk is medium or high. OSINT pipeline identifies 0 users with credentials in breach databases and elevates their risk tier, triggering CA009 on next sign-in. NIST SP 800-63B Section 5.1.1.2 requires checking passwords against known compromised lists.
Evidence: CA009 — passwordChange grant control on medium/high user risk

Controls.Title: Re-Authentication
Controls.Finding: CA006 enforces re-authentication every 8 hours for all users. CA007 enforces re-authentication every 30 minutes for privileged IT accounts, meeting the NIST SP 800-63B AAL2 recommendation for privileged sessions. Persistent browser sessions disabled in both policies.

Controls.Title: Risk Assessment
Controls.Finding: Automated risk assessment runs every 15 minutes. All 500 users classified into 4 tiers (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW) using multi-signal correlation: Entra IdP risk, MFA gaps, device compliance, OSINT exposure, and RiskScore. Current state: 0 CRITICAL, 0 HIGH.

Controls.Title: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning
Controls.Finding: Four-tool OSINT pipeline scans all 500 users every 6 hours: HIBP (12B+ records), DeHashed (credential dumps), LeakCheck (7B+ records), Intelligence X (dark web/paste sites). Results fed into risk tier classification and dashboard recommendations.
Evidence: IntelX — dark web / paste site monitoring

Controls.Title: Information System Monitoring
Controls.Finding: Entra ID Identity Protection provides real-time user and sign-in risk monitoring. GitHub Actions workflow polls Entra every 15 minutes for risky user signals. OSINT pipeline provides external credential exposure monitoring every 6 hours. All signals aggregated into central dashboard with alert banner for AT RISK posture.
Evidence: Dashboard alert banner — real-time posture indicator

Controls.Title: Configuration Settings
Controls.Finding: All security configurations defined as code in version-controlled PowerShell and Python scripts. CA policies, PIM assignments, and user provisioning are reproducible and auditable. Configuration drift detected by audit scripts comparing expected vs actual state.

Controls.Title: Authentication Assurance Level 1
Controls.Finding: AAL1 (password only) is not used for any MediZuva application. CA001 requires MFA (AAL2 minimum) for all users and all applications.
Evidence: CA001 — MFA required for All applications

Controls.Title: Authentication Assurance Level 2 (MFA Required)
Controls.Finding: AAL2 requires phishing-resistant or approved MFA. CA001 enforces MFA for all users. 0 of 535 users (0.0%) lack MFA registration. Microsoft Authenticator (OATH TOTP / push notification) satisfies AAL2.

Controls.Title: Authentication Assurance Level 3 (Hardware MFA)
Controls.Finding: AAL3 requires hardware-based phishing-resistant authenticators (FIDO2/WebAuthn). Current implementation uses Microsoft Authenticator app (AAL2). CA005 enforces MFA + compliant device for admins, partially meeting AAL3 intent. FIDO2 security keys not yet mandated for privileged accounts.
Controls.Recommendation: Configure CA005 to require FIDO2/Windows Hello for Business for IT administrator accounts to achieve AAL3.
Evidence: CA005 — MFA + compliant device for admins (AAL2.5)

Controls.Title: Memorised Secret (Password) Requirements
Controls.Finding: NIST 800-63B requires: minimum 8 characters, check against breached lists, no mandatory complexity or rotation unless compromised. CA009 forces password change when user risk is medium/high (breach-triggered rotation). Entra ID Password Protection configured with banned-password list. No arbitrary periodic rotation enforced (compliant with NIST guidance).
Evidence: CA009 — passwordChange on medium/high user risk
OSINT pipeline — breach detection feeds user risk elevation

Controls.Title: Re-Authentication Requirements
Controls.Finding: NIST 800-63B requires re-authentication after 30 minutes of inactivity for AAL2 and no more than 12 hours of continuous use. CA007 enforces 30-minute re-authentication for privileged accounts (AAL2 compliant). CA006 enforces 8-hour limit for all users (within 12-hour maximum). Persistent browser sessions disabled on all policies.
Evidence: persistentBrowser: never on all session policies

Controls.Title: Continuous Monitoring Strategy
Controls.Finding: Monitoring strategy defined across two cadences: 15-minute automated Entra ID signal refresh (threat posture) and 6-hour OSINT credential exposure scan (external intelligence). GitHub Actions implements both as automated, unattended workflows.

Controls.Title: Monitoring Frequency and Metrics
Controls.Finding: Security metrics published to central dashboard on every refresh cycle: risk tier counts (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), MFA gaps, device gaps, OSINT exposure rate, dark web exposure rate, and 6-month trend prediction. All metrics version-controlled in JSON for historical comparison.

Controls.Title: Respond to Findings
Controls.Finding: Threat intelligence engine generates prioritised recommendations (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW) on every OSINT scan, surfaced directly in the dashboard OSINT panel. Risk tier classification automatically elevates users in real time. Alert banner activates on dashboard when posture is AT RISK.
Evidence: Dashboard alert banner — AT RISK trigger
